requirements.text: WHEN listing projects, the system shall include projects from global registry entries and auto-discovered local configurations that are inside configured discovery paths.
WHEN a project has both registry metadata and local configuration, the system shall return a merged project view using local configuration for operational fields.
WHEN creating or registering a global-only project, the system shall persist the complete project definition in the global registry without requiring a local config file.
WHEN creating or registering a project-first project, the system shall persist discovery metadata in the global registry and operational fields in the project-local config.
WHEN creating or discovering an auto-discovery project, the system shall use the project-local config as the complete project definition without requiring a global registry entry.
WHEN updating editable project fields, the system shall write name, description, repository, and supported active-state changes to the source of truth for the project's configuration mode.
WHEN updating only a project description, the system shall persist the new description and return it on the next project read.
WHEN updating a visible project that has no registry file but has a local config path, the system shall update the local config instead of failing because the registry file is missing.
WHEN a project update succeeds, the system shall refresh public project reads so reopened UI forms and selectors show the persisted values.
WHEN a project is created or updated through one public interface, the system shall make the resulting project state observable through Web UI, API, CLI, and MCP project reads.
IF project input validation fails, THEN the system shall reject the operation with field-specific validation details and without writing partial configuration.
IF a project cannot be found by the supplied identifier, THEN the system shall return a not-found result that identifies the unresolved project key.
The system shall validate project codes, names, paths, and editable fields before mutating configuration files.
Project updates shall not convert a project-first minimal registry entry into a complete registry definition unless an explicit migration operation is requested.

IF registry metadata and local configuration disagree on operational project fields, THEN the system shall prefer local configuration for project-first reads and surface the inconsistency for diagnostics.
IF a file-system write fails during project update, THEN the system shall preserve the previous project configuration and report the failed write target.
The system shall preserve distinct write ownership rules for global-only, project-first, and auto-discovery projects, with configuration mode represented explicitly before write routing.
The system shall use a shared project contract for Web UI, API, CLI, and MCP project operations, including one shared mutation boundary for public project writes.
Project update tests shall verify persisted readback across the global-only, project-first, and auto-discovery mode matrix, not only successful command responses or UI success messages.
Project configuration mode shall be a first-class value with supported values global-only, project-first, and auto-discovery.
Public project mutations shall enter through one shared project application boundary before mode-specific configuration persistence is selected.

WHEN a project is read or prepared for mutation by code, id, or path, the system shall resolve one canonical project instance and, for mutations, expose enough storage ownership context to route the write before returning or mutating project data.
Project identity resolution for mutations shall expose enough storage ownership context to route writes without interface-specific configuration-mode inference.

scenarios.title: Canonical project read resolves one project instance
scenarios.given: Given a project can be found by code, id, or path and has registry metadata plus local configuration
scenarios.when: When I request the project through a public project read
scenarios.then: Then I receive one project record with operational fields from local configuration

scenarios.title: Project list includes registered and discovered projects
scenarios.given: Given one project is registered globally and another project is discoverable from local configuration in a configured search path
scenarios.then: Then I see both projects in the project list

scenarios.title: Global-only project stores and reads from registry
scenarios.given: Given I create or register a project in global-only mode
scenarios.when: When I read the project after creation
scenarios.then: Then the project is available without requiring a project-local config file

scenarios.title: Project-first project merges registry and local config
scenarios.given: Given I create or register a project-first project
scenarios.when: When I read the project after creation
scenarios.then: Then discovery metadata comes from the registry and operational fields come from the project-local config

scenarios.title: Auto-discovered project works without registry entry
scenarios.given: Given a project has a valid local config inside configured discovery paths and no global registry entry
scenarios.when: When I list or read projects
scenarios.then: Then the project is available from its local config

scenarios.title: Editable project fields update in the mode source of truth
scenarios.given: Given projects exist in global-only, project-first, and auto-discovery modes
scenarios.when: When I update project name, description, repository, or supported active state
scenarios.then: Then each project persists the changed fields in that mode's source of truth

scenarios.title: Description-only update persists and refreshes visible state
scenarios.given: Given I am editing a project description from the Web UI or API
scenarios.when: When I save only the description and reopen or reread the project
scenarios.then: Then the saved description is shown in the project read, selector, and edit form

scenarios.title: Visible project without registry file updates local config
scenarios.given: Given a visible project has a local config path but no registry file for its id
scenarios.when: When I update editable project fields
scenarios.then: Then the update succeeds by writing the local config instead of failing on the missing registry file

scenarios.title: Project updates are visible across public interfaces
scenarios.given: Given a project is created or updated through one public interface
scenarios.when: When I read the same project through Web UI, API, CLI, and MCP project reads
scenarios.then: Then each interface shows the same project state

scenarios.title: Invalid or missing project updates return clear errors
scenarios.given: Given I submit invalid project data or a project identifier that cannot be resolved
scenarios.when: When I request a project mutation or read
scenarios.then: Then the operation fails without partial writes and reports the invalid field or unresolved project key

obligations.title: ProjectService is the single shared contract for project reads, updates, cache invalidation, and current-project resolution.
ProjectConfigService owns registry/local write targeting for global-only, project-first, and auto-discovery modes.
Project mutations validate project fields through the shared validator before configuration files are mutated.

The project update API resolves the visible project instance, validates input, writes through ProjectService, falls back to local config by path when registry update is unavailable, and rereads fresh project data.
The Web edit form emits project change events after successful update and project state refresh keeps selected project data in sync for selectors and reopened forms.
CLI and MCP project reads must consume the shared ProjectService read contract rather than implementing independent project merge semantics.
Regression coverage must assert persisted readback for API and browser project edits, including description-only updates and missing-registry fallback.
CLI and MCP coverage must verify project reads expose the same shared project state after project configuration changes.
Configuration write failures must be reported with the failed target and must not leave partially mutated project state.
Project writes resolve an explicit configuration mode and write reference before persistence
Public project mutations route through one shared project mutation boundary before adapter responses are built
Project contract coverage exercises create/register, read, update, and readback across all configuration modes

artifacts.sourceRef: MDT-077 architecture web project state owner

testPlans.sourceRef: MDT-077 tests: MCP project read consistency

testPlans.title: Project validation tests cover code, name, path, tickets path, repository, and description validation before writes
testPlans.sourceRef: MDT-077 tests: shared project validation boundary

testPlans.title: Project API read/update contract covers description updates, missing-registry fallback, validation, and readback
testPlans.sourceRef: MDT-077 tests: API project update/readback contract

testPlans.title: Project contract matrix verifies create/register, read, update, and readback across global-only, project-first, and auto-discovery modes

tasks.title: Introduce explicit project mode and canonical write-reference contract
Route public project updates through one shared mutation boundary
Add table-driven project mode contract tests and read contract coverage
